title: The nature of neural object representations during dynamic occlusion
description: This magnetoencephalography (MEG) dataset investigates neural object representations during dynamic visual occlusion. Participants viewed objects that either disappeared or became occluded during MEG recording, with concurrent eye-tracking. The dataset includes raw MEG data, behavioral event logs, eye-tracking recordings, and preprocessed MEG data epoched relative to stimulus onset and position changes, enabling investigation of how the brain maintains object representations when visual input is disrupted.
methods_description: MEG data were collected while participants viewed objects undergoing dynamic occlusion or disappearance. Behavioral events and stimulus positions were recorded for each trial. Eye-tracking data (pupil position and size) were simultaneously acquired during the MEG session. Data were preprocessed and epoched relative to stimulus onset and position changes using CoSMoMVPA formatting.
